data.title: Status metrics

data.description:
Short summary
-------------
Add unit tests that verify the proxy returns a 429 text/plain response (no Retry-After header) when llama-server /slots reports 0 available slots but total_slots > 0 for chat/completions requests.

Background / context
--------------------
The slot-check logic lives in proxy/proxy/server.py. When available_slots == 0 and total_slots > 0 the current code logs a warning and raises HTTPException(status_code=503, ...). The new behavior should return a PlainTextResponse with status 429 and no Retry-After header.

Test location
-------------
Add unit tests under proxy/tests/, e.g. proxy/tests/test_slots_busy_unit.py.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Given the llama-server /slots endpoint returns a list where all items are busy (available_slots == 0 and total_slots > 0), a POST to /v1/chat/completions must result in:
  - HTTP status 429
  - Content-Type: text/plain
  - Body matching: "Model server busy: 0/<total_slots> slots available. Retry later." (the numeric total_slots must be present)
  - No `Retry-After` header present in the response
  - The `active_queries` counter is decremented (no leaked reservation)

- Tests are automated with pytest and placed under proxy/tests/ and run by the project's test runner.

Minimal implementation (smallest end-to-end slice)
--------------------------------------------------
- Add a pytest unit test that monkeypatches the httpx client used by proxy (or the module-level `_http_client`) so that a GET to /slots returns status 200 and a JSON array with all `is_processing: true`.
- Use TestClient or call the FastAPI app handler to POST to /v1/chat/completions and assert the conditions above.

Related files
-------------
- proxy/proxy/server.py (slot-check branch)
- proxy/tests/ (new test file)

Notes / implementation hints
---------------------------
- Prefer monkeypatching `_http_client` (module-level client) to avoid starting a real server.
- Ensure the test runs quickly and only exercises the slot-exhausted branch.
- Keep test deterministic and self-contained.

Verify observability signals for the slot-exhausted rejection path remain intact. In particular, ensure any existing counters or metrics used to track concurrency or rejections are still incremented when the proxy rejects requests due to slot exhaustion.

## Acceptance Criteria

- The slot-exhausted code path must continue to emit the same observability signals it did before the change. Specifically:
  - If there is an existing metric/counter for concurrency rejections (e.g., `concurrency_rejects` or a similarly named Prometheus counter), the code path must continue to increment it.
  - If no explicit counter exists in the repository, the test should verify that the proxy logs a clear warning message (example: "No available slots (<n> total), rejecting request") and record this as the observability anchor for SREs to wire alerts to.

- The test should be automated if possible. If fully-automated verification is not possible due to missing instrumentation, record the manual verification steps as part of the task's Deliverables.

data.description:
Short summary
-------------
Implement the slot-busy response change: when available_slots == 0 and total_slots > 0 for chat/completions requests, return HTTP 429 with a short plain-text body (no `Retry-After` header) rather than raising an HTTPException resulting in 503.

## Acceptance Criteria

- When the proxy detects available_slots == 0 and total_slots > 0 for a chat/completions request, it must return:
  - HTTP 429 (Too Many Requests)
  - Content-Type: text/plain
  - Body: exactly `Model server busy: 0/<total_slots> slots available. Retry later.` (the numeric total_slots must be present)
  - Must NOT include a `Retry-After` header for this case
- The model-loading 503 JSON + Retry-After behavior (via `_model_loading_response`) must remain unchanged.
- Unit and integration tests (created in sibling test work items) must pass.
- Observability signals (existing counters for concurrency rejects) must continue to be emitted (see observability task).

Minimal implementation (smallest end-to-end slice)
--------------------------------------------------
- In proxy/proxy/server.py, locate the branch that currently does:

  logger.warning("No available slots ({total_slots} total), rejecting request")
  # release active_queries
  raise HTTPException(status_code=503, detail=f"Model server busy: 0/{total_slots} slots available. Retry later.")

  and replace the raise with returning a FastAPI PlainTextResponse with status_code=429, content set to the textual message, and media_type='text/plain'.

- Preserve the existing decrement of `active_queries` and preserve any metrics/log increments.

Implementation notes
--------------------
- Use from fastapi.responses import PlainTextResponse
- Ensure the response does not include a `Retry-After` header (do not forward headers from the backend in this branch).
- Keep the `_model_loading_response` helper untouched.
- Add a small unit/integration test to validate the behavior (tests are created separately and must be implemented first).

Related files
-------------
- proxy/proxy/server.py (slot-check branch)
- proxy/tests/ (unit + integration tests created in sibling work items)
- proxy/README.md (docs to be updated separately)

Rollout / compatibility
-----------------------
- No runtime toggle (per product decision). Document the change in proxy/README.md and release notes. If customers require a toggle later, create a follow-up feature.

# Summary

Two improvements to console output during streaming:

1. **Raw JSON logging**: The console currently displays raw JSON SSE chunks which makes the streaming content scroll off screen. Only display raw JSON if --verbose is set (or verbose logging level).

2. **Prompt processing progress**: When the initial prompt is large, llama-server takes time processing input tokens (prefill phase). Show progress in the console during this phase.

## User and Player Experience
- User sees clean, readable streaming output without JSON noise
- User sees prompt processing progress (e.g., 'Processing 500/2000 tokens...') during prefill
- User can enable verbose mode to see raw JSON for debugging

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Raw JSON chunks only appear in console when log level is DEBUG or verbose flag is set
- [ ] Content-only output (just the text) appears in console by default during streaming
- [ ] Prompt processing progress is displayed in console during prefill phase
- [ ] Progress shows token count processed and optionally tokens/second
- [ ] Existing tests continue to pass

## Implementation Notes
- Modify  to log at DEBUG level for raw JSON
-  already extracts delta.content - ensure it works without raw JSON
- Parse llama-server stdout/log for prompt processing lines (e.g., 'slot N : prompt processing')
- Display progress using similar format to ContentOnlyConsoleHandler
data.title: Improve streaming console output

data.description:
# Intake Brief: Enhance Prompt Processing Progress Display

## Headline

Add real-time progress indicators during llama-server prompt processing (prefill phase), displaying token count and percentage using stdout log parsing and slot API polling.

**Work Item:** LP-0MQ064EQU004JOLY
**Priority:** High
**Type:** Feature
**Related:** LP-0MQ04EEFP006DXU9 (parent - Improve streaming console output)

---

## Problem Statement

During large prompt processing (prefill phase), users see no feedback while llama-server processes input tokens, creating uncertainty about whether the system is working or stuck. The current implementation only displays raw log lines without structured progress information.

---

## Users

**Primary:** Developer/operator using the LLM proxy via console/terminal
**User Stories:**
- As a developer, I want to see token processing progress so I know the system is working during long prefill operations
- As an operator, I want to understand processing speed so I can estimate wait times for large prompts

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Parse `progress` field from llama-server stdout log lines (format: `progress = 0.658083`)
- [ ] Display progress indicator in console with token count: `Processing 26988/40494 tokens (65%)`
- [ ] Poll `/slots` API endpoint every 500ms during prefill for real-time updates
- [ ] Show processing start indicator when prefill begins
- [ ] Show processing complete indicator when prefill ends
- [ ] Progress display uses ANSI DIM formatting (`\033[2m`) for consistency with reasoning_content
- [ ] Display updates in-place using carriage return (``) for clean progress bar style
- [ ] All existing tests continue to pass
- [ ] New tests added for progress parsing and display
- [ ] All related documentation is updated to reflect the changes, including code comments, README, and any relevant wiki or docs site entries

---

## Constraints

- Must work within the existing `_stream_output` threading model (stdout is read in a background thread)
- Must not block the streaming response to the client
- Slot API polling should be optional/configurable to avoid unnecessary load
- Progress display should gracefully handle missing or malformed progress data

## Assumptions

- llama-server `/slots` API endpoint is stable and returns consistent schema
- llama-server stdout log format for progress lines is consistent across versions
- ANSI escape codes are supported by the target terminal/console

---

## Existing State

- `_stream_output` function in `proxy/proxy/server.py` already detects "prompt processing" lines in llama-server stdout
- Current implementation writes raw log lines to stderr without parsing progress values
- `ContentOnlyConsoleHandler` handles streaming chunk display with ANSI formatting
- llama-server provides progress data in two forms:
  1. stdout logs: `prompt processing progress, n_tokens = 26988, progress = 0.658083`
  2. HTTP API: `GET /slots?model=Qwen3` returns `is_processing`, `next_token.n_decoded`

---

## Desired Change

1. **Parse progress from stdout logs:** Extract `n_tokens` and `progress` values from llama-server log lines
2. **Add slot API polling:** During active prefill, poll `/slots` endpoint every 500ms for real-time token count
3. **Format progress display:** Show `Processing X/Y tokens (Z%)` with ANSI DIM formatting
4. **In-place updates:** Use `` to overwrite the progress line for clean terminal output
5. **Completion indicator:** Show final token count when prefill completes
6. **Replace partial implementation:** Remove the incomplete progress work from LP-0MQ04EEFP006DXU9 and consolidate here

---

## Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| Scope creep (adding features beyond progress display) | Record additional feature ideas as separate work items linked to this item |
| Slot API polling adds load | Make polling configurable and only active during prefill |
| Progress data may be missing/malformed | Graceful degradation - show "Processing..." without percentages |
| Threading complexity (polling in background) | Use asyncio task with proper cancellation |

---

## Related Work

| Item | Title | Relationship |
|------|-------|--------------|
| LP-0MQ04EEFP006DXU9 | Improve streaming console output | Parent - **replaces** incomplete progress work in that item |
| LP-0MPUGXF9K000774N | Proxy: show only streamed 'content' on console | Completed - foundation for console output |
| LP-0MPHM16H9002O54V | Add proxy timeouts and concurrency limits | Related - prompt processing delays |

---

## Appendix: Clarifying Questions & Answers

- **Q:** "Progress display format: What should the progress indicator look like?"
  - **Answer (user):** "B) Token count: Processing 26988/40494 tokens (65%)"
  - **Source:** Interactive reply. Final: yes.

- **Q:** "Slot API polling: Is this required for the MVP, or should we start with just parsing stdout logs?"
  - **Answer (user):** "B) Include slot API polling for smoother updates"
  - **Source:** Interactive reply. Final: yes.

- **Q:** "Relationship to LP-0MQ04EEFP006DXU9: Should this be a child, sibling, or replace the incomplete work?"
  - **Answer (user):** "C) Replace the incomplete progress work in LP-0MQ04EEFP006DXU9"
  - **Source:** Interactive reply. Final: yes.
data.title: Enhance prompt processing progress display

data.description:
# Fallback to nearest remote free models when local slots are exhausted

Summary: When local models report zero available slots for chat/completions, attempt to forward the request to a configured remote endpoint (when enabled) to improve availability and reduce immediate client errors.

Problem statement
-----------------
When the proxy receives a chat/completions request and the local llama-server reports 0 available slots (but total_slots > 0), the request is currently rejected (503/JSON). This feature will attempt to forward the request to a nearest equivalent remote/free model (when configured) so client requests have a higher chance of succeeding instead of immediately failing.

Users
-----
- Client applications (internal services and third-party integrations) that call the proxy's OpenAI-compatible endpoints.
- Operators / SREs who want to minimise client error rates during local overload.
- Developers who implement and test fallback routing.

Example user stories
- As a client, when the local model is busy, I want the proxy to transparently try a remote model so my request completes without manual retry logic.
- As an operator, I want fallback events to be visible in metrics/logs so I can monitor how often remote fallbacks are used.

Acceptance Criteria (Success Criteria)
-------------------------------------
1. Fallback attempt: When `available_slots == 0 and total_slots > 0` for a chat/completions request, and fallback is enabled for the request (see config/clarification), the proxy MUST attempt to forward the original request to a configured remote endpoint and return the remote's response to the client (streaming or non-streaming semantics preserved). If the remote returns a success (2xx) the proxy returns that success and content unchanged (aside from normal header normalization).

2. Fallback failure handling: If the remote attempt fails (network error, 5xx, or other non-retryable condition), the proxy MUST return a clear, machine-parseable error to the client. The behaviour for aggregated failures (e.g., multiple remotes tried) is TBD pending clarification (see Appendix). If no remote fallback is available or all fallbacks fail, the proxy MUST return the existing slot-exhaustion response (currently JSON 503 with `retry_after`) unless a different default is approved in the Appendix.

3. Tests: Unit tests under `proxy/tests/` that simulate `available_slots == 0` MUST assert that the proxy attempts the configured remote fallback and that both success and failure cases behave as specified. Add integration test(s) exercising the end-to-end fallback flow (mock remote and local slots endpoint).

4. Documentation: `proxy/README.md` (and `proxy/MODEL_ADD.md` if relevant) MUST be updated to describe the new fallback behaviour, including configuration options, example curl requests/responses, and guidance for operators. The change must be discoverable in the docs and release notes.

5. Full project test suite: The new changes MUST include tests and the full project test suite must pass locally.

Notes: Any Acceptance Criteria items that depend on unanswered design choices are marked TBD in the Appendix and will be finalised after clarification.

Constraints
-----------
- Do not change the existing model-loading 503 JSON + `Retry-After` behaviour (the model-loading path must remain unchanged) unless explicitly approved in the Appendix.
- Fallback MUST only use remote endpoints configured in `proxy/config.yaml` (e.g., `default_remote` or per-model `type: remote` entries) and must not hardcode or embed secrets. API keys must be read from env or config only via existing `proxy_to_remote` code paths.
- Preserve streaming (SSE) semantics when proxying to remote streaming endpoints.
- Respect existing safety flags such as `server.llama_allow_host_fallback` and do not enable host-start fallbacks in production unless permitted by ops.
- Avoid introducing new persistent credentials or writing secrets to logs. Mask sensitive info in logs per existing patterns.

Risks & assumptions
-------------------
- Client compatibility: Some clients may depend on the current 503/`Retry-After` semantics or expect JSON responses. Mitigation: document the change, notify integrators, and if required, create a follow-up compatibility toggle (separate work item).
- Cost & quota risk: Forwarding requests to remote (third-party) providers can incur additional costs or consume shared quotas. Mitigation: gate fallback behind configuration and record a monitoring/ops task to track cost and rate-limit fallbacks if needed.
- Latency/regression: Remote fallbacks add network round-trips and may increase request latency. Mitigation: keep fallback as last-resort, use short per-request timeouts, and add metrics for latency and fallback success rate.
- Security/credentials: Remote endpoints require API keys; ensure keys are read from env/config and not leaked in logs. Mitigation: reuse `proxy_to_remote` existing API key handling and log-masking patterns.
- Scope creep: Features like health-based "nearest" selection, multi-remote failover, or dynamic ranking are out-of-scope for this item. Mitigation: record such enhancements as separate child work items linked to this item.
- Assumption: The remote endpoint(s) used for fallback expose compatible OpenAI-style semantics (or acceptable compatibility) for the proxied paths. If not, additional adapters will be needed (new work item).

Existing state
--------------
- Current behaviour: `proxy/proxy/server.py` performs a `/slots` query to the local llama-server. When `available_slots == 0 and total_slots > 0` it currently returns a JSON 503 with `retry_after` and a `Retry-After` header (see code around the slots check).
- There is an existing `proxy_to_remote(request, path, model_config)` helper that implements forwarding to remote endpoints and handles API keys from config/env.
- `proxy/config.yaml` already contains `default_remote` and per-model `type: remote` entries which can be used as fallback targets.
- There are existing unit and integration tests that exercise slot and backend resilience behaviours under `proxy/tests/`.

Desired change (implementation notes)
------------------------------------
High-level approach (conservative):
1. Add configuration gating (if required): choose one of the strategies in the Appendix (default_remote-based, per-model remote mapping, or explicit new config list). If `default_remote.enabled` is true, use that as the simplest initial behaviour (recommended default). If per-model remotes are present and match the requested model, prefer per-model remotes.

2. Modify the slot-exhaustion branch in `proxy/proxy/server.py`: when `available_slots == 0 and total_slots > 0` and fallback is enabled for the request, attempt to proxy the original request to the selected remote target using the existing `proxy_to_remote` path. Preserve streaming vs non-streaming behaviour.

3. Instrumentation & headers: add an outgoing header such as `X-Fallback-Target: <id>` and/or `X-Session-Fallback-Reason: no_slots` when a fallback occurs to aid observability; increment a new metric `fallback_total{reason="no_slots"}` (or reuse an existing metric if one exists). Exact header/metric names to be finalised in Appendix.

4. Tests: Add unit tests that patch the local `/slots` call to return 0 available slots and verify the proxy calls `proxy_to_remote` and returns the remote response in success and failure scenarios. Add integration tests that run a test HTTP server as the remote endpoint to validate streaming and non-streaming flows.

5. Docs: Update `proxy/README.md` and `proxy/MODEL_ADD.md` to document behaviour and configuration.

Related work
------------
Potentially related docs (repo paths):
- proxy/proxy/server.py — slot-check & proxy_to_remote implementation (primary change location)
- proxy/config.yaml — default_remote and per-model remote declarations
- proxy/README.md — existing docs about model-loading 503 + retry_after and router behaviour
- proxy/MODEL_ADD.md — guidance for adding remote model entries and notes about fallback and endpoints

Potentially related work items (selected):
- Return informative 503 with Retry-After and machine-readable body when no slots available — LP-0MPQ2PLTC000GYRZ (related: current slot-exhaustion semantics and tests)
- Slot-busy unit tests — LP-0MPYMV7MV002XBAY (tests created for slot-busy cases)
- Slot-busy integration test — LP-0MPYMVA04005ICQD
- Document slot-busy 429 semantics — LP-0MPYMVL550021369

Related work (automated seeds)
- LP-0MPQ2PLTC000GYRZ — Return informative 503 with Retry-After and machine-readable body when no slots available. Why related: contains current slot-exhaustion design, tests and docs tasks and should be consulted/linked so we avoid conflicting semantics.
- proxy/proxy/server.py — the exact code path to change.
- proxy/config.yaml — contains `default_remote` and per-model remote entries usage examples.

Related work (automated report)
- LP-0MPQ2PLTC000GYRZ — Return informative 503 with Retry-After and machine-readable body when no slots available (open). Why related: canonical work item that captured slot-exhaustion semantics, tests, and documentation tasks; coordinate changes to avoid conflicting client-facing behaviour.
- LP-0MPYMVHXU0098LOB — Return 429 text/plain for slot-busy (feature, child of LP-0MPQ2PLTC000GYRZ). Why related: specifically targets what the client sees when slots are exhausted and may overlap with fallback choices for error semantics.
- LP-0MPYMV7MV002XBAY / LP-0MPYMVA04005ICQD — Slot-busy unit & integration tests (tasks). Why related: existing test seeds that should be extended to cover fallback success/failure paths.
- proxy/MODEL_ADD.md — Why related: documents how to add remote model entries and the expectations for remote endpoints and keys; useful for documenting/configuring fallback targets.
- proxy/README.md — Why related: current docs describe model-loading 503 + retry_after semantics and router behaviour; must be updated to describe fallback behaviour and operator guidance.
- proxy/proxy/server.py — Why related: contains the slot-check logic and `proxy_to_remote` helper; this file is the primary change location for implementation.

Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers
---------------------------------------
- Q: "Which remote selection strategy should the fallback use? Choose one or provide a different approach:
  A) Use `default_remote` (config.default_remote) when `default_remote.enabled == true` (simple),
  B) Prefer a per-model remote mapping (`models.<name>.type == 'remote'` / alias match) when available, falling back to default_remote if not,
  C) Query a list of configured `providers` / remote hosts and select the "nearest" by latency/health (requires health-check/selection logic), or
  D) Use an explicit ordered `server.remote_fallback` list in config (new configurable list).
  " — Answer: OPEN QUESTION (no user reply yet). Source: agent interview.

- Q: "Should fallback be automatic when `default_remote.enabled` is true, or should we require an explicit config flag to enable runtime fallback? Options:
  A) Automatic when `default_remote.enabled` is true (recommended),
  B) Add `server.remote_fallback_enabled: true/false` to gate the behaviour (opt-in/opt-out), or
  C) Require an explicit per-model `allow_fallback` config field (opt-in per model).
  " — Answer: OPEN QUESTION (no user reply yet). Source: agent interview.

- Q: "Should the proxy add observable signals when fallback occurs? Options:
  A) Add outgoing header `X-Fallback-Target` and increment a metric `fallback_total{reason="no_slots"}` (recommended),
  B) Add only a log warning and leave metrics/headers to a follow-up observability task, or
  C) Do not add any new headers/metrics.
  " — Answer: OPEN QUESTION (no user reply yet). Source: agent interview.

Research performed by agent: inspected `proxy/proxy/server.py` for slot-exhaustion code path, reviewed `proxy/config.yaml` for `default_remote` and per-model remotes, and searched Worklog for existing slot-related items (LP-0MPQ2PLTC000GYRZ and related child test items). See "Related work" above for links/IDs.

# Intake Brief: Web Based Logging Per Slot (LP-0MQ0PYH8P008DLPJ)

## Headline

Add a tabbed web log viewer with per-slot progress tracking and a server-wide log area, replacing the current interleaved two-pane layout.

---

## Problem Statement

The current web log viewer (`/logs`) displays proxy and llama-server logs in two interleaved panes, making it difficult to track progress for individual slots during concurrent requests. Users need a slot-centric view to understand processing state at a glance.

---

## Users

**Primary:** Developer/operator monitoring LLM proxy and llama-server activity via web browser

**User Stories:**
- As a developer, I want to see progress for each active slot separately so I can track individual request processing without interleaving confusion
- As an operator, I want to see server-wide log messages (startup, errors, model loads) in one place so I can quickly identify system-level issues
- As a user, I want to maintain visibility into request counts and token usage per slot and in aggregate

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The `/logs` page displays a tabbed interface with a "Raw" tab and one tab per active slot
- [ ] The "Raw" tab preserves existing behavior: two-pane log view (proxy + llama) with SSE streaming
- [ ] The "Raw" tab shows aggregated request counts and token counts (current summary behavior)
- [ ] Each slot tab displays: model name, status (Idle/Processing), token count, progress percentage, queue depth
- [ ] Slot tabs update in real-time via SSE or polling from `/slots` API
- [ ] A 7-line scrollable global log area is displayed above all tabs, showing server-wide messages (proxy startup, errors, model loads)
- [ ] Global log area receives filtered SSE stream of server-wide events
- [ ] Tab count dynamically updates as slots become active/inactive
- [ ] Existing tests continue to pass
- [ ] New tests added for slot tab rendering and global log filtering
- [ ] All related documentation is updated to reflect the changes, including code comments, README, and any relevant wiki or docs site entries
- [ ] Full project test suite must pass with the new changes

---

## Constraints

- Must work within the existing SSE-based log streaming architecture
- Slot data must come from the `/slots` API endpoint (already polled by the proxy)
- Global log filtering must not introduce significant latency to log delivery
- Tab UI must be responsive and work on typical developer screen sizes (1280px+)

---

## Risks & Assumptions

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| Scope creep (adding features beyond slot tabs) | Record additional feature ideas as separate work items linked to this item |
| Slot API polling frequency affects performance | Use existing polling infrastructure; configurable interval |
| Dynamic tab count may cause UI jank with rapid slot changes | Debounce tab creation/removal; show placeholder for unknown slots |
| Global log filtering may miss important messages | Allow "show all" toggle in global area |

---

## Assumptions

- llama-server `/slots` API is stable and returns consistent schema
- Existing slot polling infrastructure can be reused for web UI updates
- Server-wide log messages can be identified by log source or message pattern

---

## Existing State

- `/logs` endpoint in `proxy/proxy/server.py` renders inline HTML with two-pane log viewer
- SSE streaming via `/logs/tail` for both proxy and llama logs
- Request counts and token counts displayed in summary section
- `slot_polling_state` dict tracks `is_processing` and `n_decoded` per model
- Console output already shows per-slot progress (prefill phase)

---

## Desired Change

1. **Restructure `/logs` HTML** to use a tabbed layout
2. **Create "Raw" tab** preserving existing two-pane view + summary metrics
3. **Create per-slot tabs** that poll `/slots` API and display slot-specific progress
4. **Add global log area** with filtered SSE stream for server-wide messages
5. **Dynamic tab management** as slots become active/inactive

---

## Related Work

| Item | Title | Relationship |
|------|-------|--------------|
| LP-0MQ064EQU004JOLY | Enhance prompt processing progress display | Completed - slot polling infrastructure |
| LP-0MQ0QR70T004EGD0 | In-place progress display | Completed - progress formatting |
| LP-0MPUGXF9K000774N | Proxy: show only streamed 'content' on console | Completed - console output pattern |
| LP-0MQ04EEFP006DXU9 | Improve streaming console output | Parent - streaming output improvements |

---

## Appendix: Clarifying Questions & Answers

- **Q:** "What progress information should be shown on each slot tab?"
  - **Answer (user):** "Token count + percentage + slot details (model name, queue depth) + status (Idle, Processing...)"
  - **Source:** Interactive reply. Final: yes.

- **Q:** "How should the global log area (7-line scrollable) update?"
  - **Answer (user):** "SSE stream filtered for server-wide messages (proxy startup, errors, model loads)"
  - **Source:** Interactive reply. Final: yes.

- **Q:** "What should happen to the existing Request Summary (counts + tokens) section?"
  - **Answer (user):** "Maintain per-slot metrics and show totals on the Raw tab"
  - **Source:** Interactive reply. Final: yes.

---

## Related work (automated report)

### Work Items

| ID | Title | Status | Relevance |
|----|-------|--------|-----------|
| LP-0MQ064EQU004JOLY | Enhance prompt processing progress display | completed | **Infrastructure** - Provides slot polling and progress parsing that this feature builds upon |
| LP-0MQ0QR70T004EGD0 | In-place progress display | completed | **UI pattern** - Establishes in-place display conventions for progress information |
| LP-0MQ04EEFP006DXU9 | Improve streaming console output | completed | **Parent context** - Streaming output improvements that the web UI extends |
| LP-0MPUGXF9K000774N | Proxy: show only streamed 'content' on console | completed | **Foundation** - ContentOnlyConsoleHandler pattern used for console output |

### Code References

- `proxy/proxy/server.py` — `/logs` endpoint (current two-pane viewer), `/logs/tail` SSE, slot polling
- `proxy/proxy/server.py` — `slot_polling_state` dict tracks per-model slot status
- `proxy/proxy/server.py` — `poll_slots_for_model()` polls `/slots` API

### External References

- llama-server `/slots` API endpoint for real-time slot status
- llama-server stdout log format: `slot N : prompt processing progress, n_tokens = X, progress = Y`

---

*Intake brief prepared by Map. Ready for planning.*

## Unit tests: fallback routing

Add unit tests under `proxy/tests/` that verify the fallback routing behaviour when local slots are exhausted.

### Summary
Unit tests verifying fallback triggers on slot exhaustion, selects correct remote, and returns remote response (success & failure).

### User Story
As a developer, I want automated tests that validate fallback routing so I can verify the feature works correctly and regressions are caught early.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Tests pass under `proxy/tests/` with `pytest`
2. Mock `/slots` endpoint returns 0 available slots (total_slots > 0)
3. `proxy_to_remote` is called with the correct fallback target (per-model remote preferred, then default_remote)
4. Success case: remote returns 2xx → proxy returns 2xx with remote content unchanged
5. Failure case: remote returns error → proxy returns existing slot-exhaustion error (503 with retry_after)

## Minimal Implementation
- Add tests under `proxy/tests/` using `unittest.mock` to patch slot check and `proxy_to_remote`

- Test both success and failure remote responses

## Remote selection logic

Implement per-model remote preference with `default_remote` fallback.

### Summary
Implement a `resolve_fallback_target()` function that selects the appropriate remote endpoint for fallback routing, preferring per-model remotes and falling back to `default_remote`.

### User Story
As an operator, I want fallback routing to prefer per-model remote mappings so I can control which remote endpoint handles overflow for each local model.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. `resolve_fallback_target(request_model, config)` returns per-model remote config if `models.<name>.type == 'remote'`
2. Falls back to `default_remote` if `default_remote.enabled == true`
3. Returns `None` when no remote is configured or enabled
4. Function is pure and testable (no side effects)

## Minimal Implementation
- Add `resolve_fallback_target(request_model, config)` function in `proxy/proxy/server.py`
- Check per-model `type: remote` entries first (match by model name or alias)
- Fall back to `default_remote` if `enabled == true`
- Return `None` if no remote available

## Dependencies
- None (pure function, no prerequisites)

## Deliverables
- Function `resolve_fallback_target()` in `server.py`
- Unit tests validating all branches

## Slot-exhaustion fallback

Modify the slot-exhaustion branch to forward requests via `proxy_to_remote` when a fallback target is available.

### Summary
When `available_slots == 0` and `total_slots > 0`, attempt to forward the request to the selected remote target instead of immediately rejecting.

### User Story
As a client application, when the local model is busy, I want the proxy to transparently try a remote model so my request completes without manual retry logic.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. When `available_slots == 0` and `total_slots > 0` and fallback is enabled, proxy calls `resolve_fallback_target()` to get target
2. If target exists, proxy calls `proxy_to_remote()` and returns remote response unchanged (aside from header normalization)
3. If remote fails (network error, 5xx, or other non-retryable condition), proxy returns existing slot-exhaustion error (503 with `retry_after`)
4. Streaming (SSE) semantics preserved when proxying to remote
5. No secrets leaked in logs; sensitive info masked per existing patterns

## Minimal Implementation
- Modify slot-check block in chat/completions handler (around line 2599 in `server.py`)
- Call `resolve_fallback_target(request_model, config)` when slots exhausted
- If target exists, wrap `proxy_to_remote()` call in try/except
- On success, return remote response
- On failure, fall back to existing 503 response
- Preserve streaming vs non-streaming behaviour

## Dependencies
- Depends on F1 (Remote selection logic)

## Deliverables
- Modified slot-exhaustion branch in `proxy/proxy/server.py`
- Existing tests still pass
data.title: Slot-exhaustion fallback

## Observability: header + metric

Add `X-Fallback-Target` header and `fallback_total` metric when fallback occurs.

### Summary
Instrument the fallback path with an outgoing header and a metric counter for monitoring and observability.

### User Story
As an operator/SRE, I want fallback events to be visible in metrics/logs so I can monitor how often remote fallbacks are used and troubleshoot issues.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Outgoing response includes `X-Fallback-Target: <remote_id>` header when fallback occurs
2. `fallback_total{reason="no_slots"}` metric is incremented on each fallback
3. Metric is visible at the `/metrics` endpoint
4. Header does not leak sensitive information (API keys, tokens)

## Minimal Implementation
- Add `X-Fallback-Target` header to response in fallback path (after successful remote response)
- Increment `fallback_total` metric counter in metrics module
- Register `fallback_total` metric in `proxy/metrics.py`
- Ensure header value is safe (remote endpoint ID or name, not URL with credentials)

## Dependencies
- Depends on F2 (Slot-exhaustion fallback)

## Deliverables
- Header added to fallback responses
- Metric registered and incremented
- Metric visible at `/metrics`

# E2E Tabbed Logs (Playwright)

Summary

Playwright end-to-end smoke test verifying the tabbed UI (Raw tab present + preserved two-pane streaming), global log area receives server events, and per-slot tabs appear when slot data is present (slot data simulated via request stubbing).

## Acceptance Criteria

- The Playwright test opens /logs and verifies a tab bar that contains a "Raw" tab visible by default.
- The Raw tab displays the existing two-pane proxy + llama logs and the request/token summary.
- A 7-line global log area above the tabs receives and renders a sample /events SSE message (stubbed in test) and shows it within the 7-line buffer.
- Stubbing /slots responses (via route interception) causes a per-slot tab to appear with model name, status "Processing", a token count (n_decoded), and a progress percentage.
- The test runs headless and exits with success/failure return codes suitable for CI (non-flaky).

## Minimal Implementation (end-to-end slice)

- Add Playwright test file `proxy/tests/playwright/logs-tab.spec.js`.
- Use page.route or page.addInitScript to stub /slots and to deliver a small SSE mock for /events to validate rendering without requiring a real llama-server.
- The test should assert DOM elements and basic interactivity (tab switching).

## Dependencies

- Uses existing /logs page. No server changes required for the initial failing test.

## Deliverables

- Playwright test file `proxy/tests/playwright/logs-tab.spec.js`.
- README test instructions (how to run Playwright smoke).
- CI job snippet (optional) to run the smoke test in Chromium.

# Unit: Logs HTML structure

Summary

Pytest unit test that asserts /logs HTML contains the tabbed UI placeholders and the global log container (server-side rendering checks using TestClient).

## Acceptance Criteria

- GET /logs returns HTML containing identifiable placeholders for:
  - a global log area container (id or class),
  - a tab bar container and a Raw tab placeholder,
  - a slot-tabs container placeholder.
- The TestClient-based test passes when run locally and in CI.

## Minimal Implementation

- Add `proxy/tests/test_logs_html.py` using FastAPI TestClient to call `view_logs` and assert for strings/IDs present in the response HTML.
- Keep assertions implementation-agnostic (look for id/class names and a Raw tab label).

## Dependencies

- None (test-first).

## Deliverables

- `proxy/tests/test_logs_html.py` pytest file.
data.title: Unit: Logs HTML structure

# Unit: Slot polling & /slots behavior

Summary

Async unit tests for `poll_slots_for_model` behavior and for a proxy GET `/slots?model=<name>` endpoint.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `poll_slots_for_model` updates `slot_polling_state[model]` with keys `is_processing` (bool) and `n_decoded` (int|null) for both list and dict /slots responses.
- GET `/slots?model=<name>` returns a 200 with JSON matching expected shapes (list or dict) when backend responds, and gracefully returns a 503 or safe fallback when backend is unavailable.

## Minimal Implementation

- Add `proxy/tests/test_slots_polling.py` using httpx.MockTransport or by mocking `httpx.AsyncClient` to simulate llama-server `/slots` responses and assert `slot_polling_state` updates.
- Add TestClient-based test for GET `/slots` behavior.

## Dependencies

- None (test-first).

## Deliverables

- `proxy/tests/test_slots_polling.py` pytest file.
data.title: Unit: Slot polling & /slots behavior

# UI: Tabbed layout (Raw preserved)

Summary

Convert `/logs` HTML into a tabbed layout with a "Raw" tab that preserves the existing two-pane logs and request/token summary.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `/logs` renders a tab bar with a visible "Raw" tab and the existing two-pane proxy/llama panes inside the Raw tab.
- Existing SSE connections to `/logs/tail` for proxy and llama continue to stream and update the Raw panes without regression.
- Existing summary metrics (counts/tokens) remain visible and updated as before.
- Unit tests for Logs HTML structure pass.

## Minimal Implementation

- Restructure `view_logs` HTML in `proxy/proxy/server.py` to add a tab container and move the current two-pane markup into the Raw tab.
- Add minimal JS for tab switching (no framework required) and CSS adjustments to maintain layout.
- Avoid changes to SSE endpoints or streaming logic in this step; keep behavior identical for Raw.

## Dependencies

- Unit: Logs HTML structure (test) must exist before implementation (TDD).

## Deliverables

- Updated `proxy/proxy/server.py` (HTML string) and any helper CSS/JS.
- Small demo screenshot or Playwright smoke check.
data.title: UI: Tabbed layout (Raw preserved)

data.description:
# UI: Slot tabs (dynamic)

Summary

Per-slot tabs that poll the `/slots` API and present model name, status (Idle/Processing), token count (`n_decoded`), progress %, and queue depth; tabs are added/removed dynamically as slots become active/inactive.

## Acceptance Criteria

- For each active slot reported by GET `/slots?model=<model>`, a corresponding tab is displayed and updated in near-real-time (polling interval configurable, default 0.5–1s).
- Each slot tab shows: model name, status (Idle/Processing), token count (n_decoded), progress percentage when available, and queue depth if available.
- Tabs are added/removed with a debounce (e.g., 500ms) to avoid UI jank during rapid changes.
- Playwright E2E test and unit tests verify tab presence and updates.

## Minimal Implementation

- Add client JS to poll GET `/slots?model=<currentModel>` (configurable interval) and render slot tabs into the tab container.
- Reuse existing `slot_polling_state` shape when `/slots` is proxied or available.
- Implement a debounce for tab add/remove and a placeholder for unknown slots.

## Dependencies

- Backend `/slots` proxy endpoint (feature: Backend /slots proxy) should be available; Unit tests for slot polling must exist.

## Deliverables

- JS code embedded in `/logs` page, small CSS, and Playwright assertions.
data.title: UI: Slot tabs (dynamic)

data.description:
# Backend /slots proxy

Summary

Add GET `/slots?model=<name>` endpoint that returns the slot status for the model — prefer using `slot_polling_state` if available; fallback to proxying `llama-server` `/slots`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- GET `/slots?model=<name>` returns JSON with either list-of-slots or a single-slot dict; returns 200 on success and a safe fallback (503 with a clear error) when backend is unavailable.
- `poll_slots_for_model` can be started for models on-demand and updates `slot_polling_state[model]`.
- Endpoint is non-blocking and respects timeouts.

## Minimal Implementation

- Implement a FastAPI route `/slots` in `proxy/proxy/server.py` that:
  - Accepts `model` query param
  - If `slot_polling_state.get(model)` exists, returns a normalized JSON payload built from that cache
  - Otherwise proxies to `http://localhost:<llama_port>/slots?model=<model>` with a short timeout and returns upstream response or a 503 with a JSON error
- Add tests (Unit: Slot polling & /slots behavior) to validate behavior.

## Dependencies

- Unit tests for slot polling and existing `poll_slots_for_model` behavior.

## Deliverables

- New route in `proxy/proxy/server.py` and tests under `proxy/tests`.
data.title: Backend /slots proxy

# UI: Global log area (SSE)

Summary

Add a 7-line scrollable global server log area above the tabs that subscribes to `/events` SSE and shows server-wide messages (ready, error, switching, model loads).

## Acceptance Criteria

- A 7-line global log area is visible above the tab bar on `/logs`.
- The global area receives events from `/events` EventSource and shows the last 7 server events (type + message + timestamp).
- A "Show all" toggle expands the area to view the full stream.
- Playwright E2E test verifies the SSE mock drives the UI update.

## Minimal Implementation

- Add a global log container and client JS that connects to `/events` via EventSource and appends messages filtered by `type` (error/ready/switching) into the 7-line rolling buffer.
- Include a simple "Show all" toggle.

## Dependencies

- Uses existing `/events` SSE endpoint (already present in `server.py`).

## Deliverables

- HTML/JS changes in `/logs` and Playwright assertions.
data.title: UI: Global log area (SSE)

# Backend: SSE & telemetry improvements

Summary

Ensure `broadcast_status` events include structured fields and add a timestamp; add minimal telemetry counters for model load/switch and slot active/inactive transitions to `metrics`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `/events` messages include a `type` field, a `message` or structured payload, and an ISO8601 `timestamp`.
- Metrics counters increment on model load success/failure and when slots become active/inactive (exposed via `/admin/metrics`).
- Unit tests verify the event structure and that metrics are updated when the corresponding functions are invoked.

## Minimal Implementation

- Normalize `broadcast_status` calls to include `timestamp` and a consistent structure (e.g. `{type, message, timestamp, details}`).
- Add metrics counters in `metrics.py` and increment them in relevant places (e.g. after `ensure_model_loaded` success/failure and when `slot_polling_state` toggles is_processing).

## Dependencies

- Small code changes in `proxy/proxy/server.py` and `proxy/metrics.py`.

## Deliverables

- Code changes, unit tests, and updated admin/metrics documentation.
data.title: Backend: SSE & telemetry improvements

data.description:
# Detect and self-heal llama-server GPU crash states via health endpoint

## Problem statement

The llama-server worker process crashed with a ROCm GPU error (unspecified launch failure), leaving the service in a degraded state where the proxy returned 500 Internal Server Errors. The /health endpoint incorrectly reported the service as healthy despite the backend being unreachable, requiring manual intervention to restart the services.

## Root Cause Analysis

### Timeline of Events

1. **ROCm GPU Crash**: The llama-server worker process (PID 3294171) crashed with `ROCm error: unspecified launch failure` at `ggml-cuda.cu:97` during `hipStreamSynchronize()`.
2. **Zombie Process**: The worker became a zombie process (State: Z) while the main router process (PID 3294081) continued running.
3. **Connection Failures**: The router attempted to proxy requests to the crashed worker, resulting in repeated "Could not establish connection" errors.
4. **500 Errors**: The proxy returned 500 Internal Server Errors because the backend was unreachable.
5. **Incorrect Health Status**: The /health endpoint continued to report `"status":"healthy"` despite the backend being down.

### Process State at Time of Failure

- Main router (PID 3294081): Running (sleeping)
- Worker process (PID 3294171): Zombie (defunct)
- New worker (PID 50907): Failed to establish GPU connection

### Evidence from Logs

```
[50907] ROCm error: unspecified launch failure
[50907] hipStreamSynchronize(cuda_ctx->stream())
srv operator(): http client error: Failed to read connection
srv operator(): http client error: Could not establish connection
srv log_server_r: done request: POST /v1/chat/completions 127.0.0.1 500
```

## Users

- **Primary**: On-call engineers who need to detect and respond to GPU failures quickly.
- **Secondary**: Developers who need accurate health signals to know when to retry or wait.
- **Tertiary**: Automated systems that rely on /health for load balancing or circuit breaking.

### User Stories

- As an operator, I want /health to accurately reflect backend availability so I can detect issues without manual investigation.
- As an operator, I want the system to automatically recover from transient GPU failures without requiring manual restart.
- As a developer, when the system is self-healing, I want a clear error response (503 with Retry-After: 30) so I know an error was detected and the team is working on it.
- As a developer, I want clear error signals (503 vs 500) so I can distinguish between "service unavailable" and "service broken".

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] /health endpoint returns `"status": "degraded"` (not `"healthy"`) when the llama-server worker process has crashed or is unreachable, even if the main router process is still running.
- [ ] /health endpoint includes a new field `"backend_reachable": false` when backend connection tests fail.
- [ ] The watchdog detects worker process crashes (zombie/defunct state) in addition to main process crashes.
- [ ] The system attempts automatic restart when a worker crash is detected in router mode.
- [ ] When self-healing is actively in progress, requests receive a 503 response with `Retry-After: 30` header and a message: "Backend error detected, team is working on recovery. Please retry after 30 seconds."
- [ ] When self-healing is NOT in progress (e.g., backend is down but no restart attempt is running), existing error responses (500) remain unchanged.
- [ ] Automatic restart includes exponential backoff with a maximum of 3 restart attempts within 5 minutes before giving up and requiring manual intervention.
- [ ] All restart attempts and failures are logged with clear messages for triage.
- [ ] Tests verify: (a) /health returns degraded when worker is crashed, (b) automatic restart is attempted, (c) restart backoff works correctly, (d) health recovers after successful restart, (e) 503 with Retry-After is returned during self-healing.
- [ ] All related documentation is updated to reflect the changes, including code comments, README, and any relevant wiki or docs site entries.
- [ ] Full project test suite must pass with the new changes.

## Constraints

- Must not break existing health check behavior for other failure modes.
- Must not create infinite restart loops that could overwhelm the system.
- Must respect existing container/distrobox constraints for process management.
- Automatic restart should only be attempted in router mode (consistent with existing watchdog behavior).

## Existing state

- Previous work item LP-0MPN8BALG000DBB3 implemented watchdog and health-gated readiness, but the current incident shows gaps in worker crash detection.
- The watchdog currently only monitors `llama_process.poll()` which checks the main process, not worker subprocesses.
- The /health endpoint checks `llama_process.poll() is None` which is True when the main process is alive but a worker has crashed.
- The proxy has retry/backoff logic but returns 500 errors instead of 503 when backend is unreachable.

## Desired change

1. **Enhanced Health Detection**: Add backend reachability test to /health that actively checks if the llama-server can handle requests (e.g., query /slots or /health on the backend), not just if the process is alive.
2. **Worker Crash Detection**: Modify watchdog to detect zombie/defunct child processes in addition to main process exit.
3. **Improved Self-Healing**: Implement restart with exponential backoff and maximum attempt limit.
4. **Client Communication During Self-Healing**: When self-healing is actively in progress, return 503 with `Retry-After: 30` header and message: "Backend error detected, team is working on recovery. Please retry after 30 seconds." When self-healing is not active, existing error responses remain unchanged.

## Related work

- LP-0MPN8BALG000DBB3 — Mitigate llama-server ROCm crash path and add backend recovery safeguards (previous hardening that should be extended)
- LP-0MPN836LQ002YQCY — Investigate llama-server backend connection errors causing proxy retries and concurrency spike
- `proxy/proxy/server.py` — Current health endpoint and watchdog implementation
- `proxy/tests/test_backend_resilience.py` — Existing resiliency tests to extend
- `docs/llama-router.md` — Router mode documentation

## Risks & Assumptions

- Risk: Active health probes could add latency to health checks; mitigation: make probes optional or use lightweight slot queries.
- Risk: Restart backoff might be too aggressive or too lenient; mitigation: make backoff configurable and test with fault injection.
- Risk: Zombie process detection may vary across platforms; mitigation: test on target deployment environment.
- Risk: Scope creep into broader resiliency redesign; mitigation: create linked follow-up work items for out-of-scope opportunities.
- Assumption: The main router process remains healthy when a worker crashes; if the main process also dies, existing watchdog handles that case.
- Assumption: ROCm GPU errors are typically transient and may succeed after restart; if hardware is permanently failed, restart attempts will fail and manual intervention is required.

## Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers

- Q: "Should the health check actively test backend reachability (e.g., query /slots) or rely on process status?" — Answer (user): Probe the backend and provide a JSON response with full health status. Final: yes.
- Q: "What is the maximum number of automatic restart attempts before giving up?" — Answer (user): 3 in 5 minutes is good. Final: yes.
- Q: "What should the client see during self-healing?" — Answer (user): Error response indicating error was detected, team is working on it, retry in 30 seconds. Final: yes.
- Q: "Should the system return 503 instead of 500 when backend is unreachable?" — Answer (user): Only during active self-healing, not for all backend connection failures. Final: yes.
data.title: Detect and self-heal llama-server GPU crash states via health endpoint

data.description:
Headline

Add a dev mode (CLI flag) to the proxy so developers can run a development instance with debug logging and auto-reload on alternate ports, allowing concurrent use and development.

Problem statement

We cannot both use and work on the proxy at the same time: running a development instance collides with a running production instance (ports, logs, configuration). This blocks local development, testing, and debugging. We need an opt-in dev mode that runs the proxy and any started llama-server backend on alternate ports, enables debug logging, and supports an auto-reload developer workflow.

Users

- Local developers working on the proxy codebase (start/stop, iterate, debug). Example: "As a developer, I want to run a dev proxy instance side-by-side with the system proxy so I can reproduce bugs without disrupting the running service."
- Support engineers who need to reproduce production issues in a local dev environment.
- CI / test harness authors who want a documented, repeatable dev invocation for integration tests that require a temporary local proxy instance.

Acceptance Criteria (measurable)

1. Dev mode can be enabled via a CLI flag: `proxyctl start --dev` (primary UX). When enabled, the proxy process binds the web server to http://localhost:8001 and configures the local llama-server backend to use port 8081 by default. Verification: start with `proxyctl start --dev` and confirm `GET http://localhost:8001/health` returns 200 and that backend probe uses port 8081 (logs or /health backend_reachable shows 8081).

2. Dev mode enables DEBUG logging for the proxy (runtime logger `llama-proxy` has level DEBUG) and ensures logs are written to a developer-writable location (./logs/ or $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR fallback). Verification: runtime log level asserted in a smoke test or by inspecting the running process output; logs appear in ./logs/ when not running as root.

3. Dev mode runs the server with auto-reload enabled for rapid iteration. Verification: running `proxyctl start --dev` (or equivalent uvicorn dev command) with a small file change to source files causes the server process to restart (observed by PID change or by service CLI status). Provide a simple smoke test/integration step that asserts reload works in a developer environment.

4. Documentation and CLI UX: `proxyctl` supports `start --dev` and `stop`/`status`/`logs` operate against the dev instance. `proxy/README.md` includes usage examples for dev mode and an example uvicorn command to run the proxy in dev mode: `python -m uvicorn proxy.server:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8001 --reload --log-level debug`.

5. Tests & validation: Add tests that exercise dev-mode startup behavior (e.g., `proxy/tests/test_dev_mode.py`) and ensure the full project test suite passes with the new changes. Verification: CI/local test run shows all tests passing; new dev-mode tests are present and passing.

6. All related documentation is updated to reflect the changes, including code comments, README, and any relevant wiki or docs site entries.

Constraints

- Dev mode must be opt-in via a CLI flag (`proxyctl start --dev`) — it must not be enabled by default or by accident.
- Default dev ports: proxy web server 8001, llama_server backend 8081. These are explicit defaults that must be documented and overridable via CLI flags or config overrides for developers who need different ports.
- Dev mode must avoid writing to privileged log directories (do not assume /var/log/llama-proxy is writable). Default developer log output should go to ./logs/ or the user's XDG runtime/state directories.
- The implementation should avoid changing production defaults (server.port remains 8000, llama_server_port remains 8080 unless dev mode is used).
- Implementation language: update `proxy/proxyctl` (bash) and add minimal Python/uvicorn invocation guidance; keep changes small and low-risk.

Existing state

- `proxy/config.yaml` currently defaults to server.port=8000 and llama_server_port=8080.
- `proxy/proxyctl` exists and already supports start/stop/restart/status/logs semantics; it is a natural place to add `--dev` handling.
- Tests and docs currently assume a proxy running on http://localhost:8000 (tests in `proxy/tests/*` reference 8000).
- The proxy code supports router mode and launching llama-server (docs: `docs/llama-router.md`, code: `proxy/proxy/server.py`).

Risks & assumptions

- Risk: Port collisions with an already-running system proxy or other services. Mitigation: use explicit dev ports (8001/8081) and make ports overridable; document port usage and conflicts in README.
- Risk: CI or tests assume fixed default ports (8000/8080) and will fail if dev runs inadvertently leak into the test environment. Mitigation: update tests to allow port overrides and isolate dev-mode tests; mark optional/flaky tests accordingly.
- Risk: Enabling auto-reload could cause noisy restarts or file-watch resource usage on low-RAM platforms. Mitigation: default reload to on for developer flows only and document how to disable it for constrained machines.
- Risk: Scope creep — developers may request additional dev features (containers, remote debugging, port mappings) that enlarge scope. Mitigation: record additional features and refactor requests as separate follow-up work items linked to this epic; do not expand current scope beyond the CLI flag + docs + tests.
- Assumption: Developer workflow is local host-based; containerized or remote developer workflows may require follow-up tasks.

Desired change (high level)

- Add an opt-in `--dev` flag to `proxyctl start` that:
  - Starts the proxy with `--port 8001` and configures the proxied llama-server to use port 8081 by default (allow overrides)
  - Launches the uvicorn process with `--reload` and `--log-level debug` for fast iteration
  - Writes logs to ./logs/ (or a developer runtime directory) by default
- Update `proxy/README.md` with dev-mode instructions and example direct uvicorn invocation
- Add minimal tests validating dev-mode binding, logging, and reload behaviour
- Keep production UX unchanged; ensure no configuration drift

Related work (automated collection)

Potentially related docs (file paths)
- proxy/README.md — Proxy usage, logging and config; update with dev-mode examples
- proxy/config.yaml — Server and llama_server port defaults (8000 / 8080)
- docs/llama-router.md — Notes on how the proxy starts llama-server in router mode; relevant to startup/port choices
- proxy/proxyctl — CLI that manages proxy; target for `--dev` UX
- proxy/tests/* — Existing tests assume default port 8000; they will need to accept overrides or the test harness must control ports when launching dev instances

Potentially related work items (title — id)
- Proxy CLI: manage running proxy (start/stop/restart) — LP-0MNSRGEQW000QM7P (proxyctl CLI design/implementation)
- Split logging web UI: proxy + llama-server tail — LP-0MNWAC6PM007BFWQ (llama-server logs visibility)
- Proxy: router-mode integration — LP-0MNJEG2EH00434ZT (how proxy starts/controls llama-server)
- Add session header resolution logging — LP-0MPOOT5P3007CZLF (recent logging changes; helps with verification)

Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers

- Q: "How should dev mode be enabled?
- A) CLI flag when starting the server (e.g. python -m uvicorn proxy.server:app --dev or a proxyctl start --dev)
- B) Environment variable (e.g. PROXY_DEV=1)
- C) Config file flag (proxy/config.yaml: server.dev_mode: true)
- D) Any combination (prefer CLI + env fallback)" — Answer (user): "A" (CLI flag). Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

- Q: "What should the default port behaviour be for dev mode?
- A) Fixed dev ports (suggested defaults: web server 8001, llama_server 8081)
- B) Port offset from configured port (dev_port = configured_port + 1)
- C) Keep same ports (no change) and only change logging" — Answer (user): "A" (Fixed dev ports). Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

- Q: "What dev-mode runtime behaviour should be enabled by default?
- A) Enable DEBUG logging for proxy and llama-server; logs to console and dev log dir; no auto-reload
- B) Enable DEBUG logging and auto-reload (watch code files; restart on change)
- C) Only enable debug logging when explicitly requested; otherwise leave behavior unchanged" — Answer (user): "B" (DEBUG logging + auto-reload). Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

Notes / agent inferences

- Agent inference: recommended dev ports used: web 8001, llama_server 8081 (based on earlier suggested defaults and user's selection of option A). Source: agent recommendation + user confirmation of option A.
- Agent inference: primary UX change will be implemented in `proxy/proxyctl` to minimize disruption to existing workflows and because `proxyctl` is already used in docs. Source: repo inspection (`proxy/proxyctl` exists) and prior related work (`Proxy CLI` work item).

OPEN QUESTIONS

- None at this time. If any edge-case platform needs (e.g., Windows path quirks, containerized dev flows) arise during implementation, create follow-up tasks scoped to those platforms.

Implementation notes (developer guidance)

- Primary change: update `proxy/proxyctl` to accept `--dev` (or `proxyctl start --dev`) which will:
  - set PORT=8001 and LLAMA_SERVER_PORT=8081 unless overridden by explicit flags
  - pass `--reload` and `--log-level debug` to uvicorn when running the app
  - set logging directory to ./logs/ by default for dev runs (or XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/llama-proxy)
- Secondary: update README with `proxyctl start --dev` examples and a direct uvicorn example for developers:
  - `python -m uvicorn proxy.server:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8001 --reload --log-level debug`
- Tests: add `proxy/tests/test_dev_mode.py` that:
  - launches the proxy in a subprocess with dev ports and asserts /health 200
  - checks logs show DEBUG-level messages
  - (optional) verifies that changing a watched file causes the server to restart (smoke/integration test, may be skipped in CI if flaky)
- Docs: update proxy/README.md and any CI/test scaffolding that assumes port 8000 to allow override via environment variable for test harnesses.
data.title: Proxy: add dev mode to allow simultaneous use and development

data.description:
# RCA: 500 Internal Server Errors from proxy during recent work via proxy

## Problem statement
Intermittent 500 Internal Server Errors were observed from the proxy for client POSTs to `/v1/chat/completions` during a recent session (example timestamp: 2026-06-05 22:44:34). Logs show session invalidation and a `history_mismatch` fallback around the same time; the errors caused failed completions and are tagged critical by the operator.

## Users
- Client applications (internal services and third-party integrators) that call the proxy's OpenAI-compatible endpoints.
- Operators / SREs responsible for uptime and incident response.
- Support engineers and developers diagnosing failures and applying hotfixes.

Example user stories
- As an operator, I want the proxy to stop returning intermittent 500s for chat completions so clients don't fail unexpectedly.
- As a developer, I want clear logs, a reproducible test case, and a small hotfix PR so I can fix the root cause and verify the fix.

## Acceptance Criteria (Success Criteria)
1. Root cause identification: The investigation must produce a concise root-cause statement with supporting evidence (relevant log excerpts, stack traces, request IDs or correlation IDs, and a short timeline). Evidence must include at least one reproducer (unit/integration test or repro script) or a clear explanation why reproduction is not possible.
2. Hotfix PR & deployment: A hotfix PR addressing the root cause must be opened (and merged if approved) and include tests that exercise the failure path. A deployment plan (rollout + rollback) must be documented; if permitted by ops, the hotfix is deployed to production (or staging with prod-like load) and verified by reproducing the original failing request pattern with no 500 responses.
3. Tests & validation: Unit and/or integration tests that reproduce the failure mode (or simulate the causal conditions) are added under `proxy/tests/` and verified locally. Full project test suite must pass with the new changes.
4. Monitoring & alerting: A monitoring rule or alert is created to detect recurrence (e.g., sudden increase in 5xx for `/v1/chat/completions` or upstream errors). Dashboard or metric increments and a short runbook for on-call are provided. If thresholds are required, list them in the runbook (TBD: confirm exact thresholds).
5. Documentation: All related documentation is updated to reflect the remediation and any changed behaviours, including code comments, `proxy/README.md`, and any relevant runbook or wiki pages.

Notes: If any Acceptance Criterion requires clarification (for example exact alert thresholds or deployment window), the Appendix lists the open questions and will be used to finalize the criterion.

## Constraints
- Minimise customer-facing changes: avoid changing client-visible API semantics unless necessary for correctness and agreed with stakeholders.
- Avoid leaking secrets in logs; follow existing log-masking patterns.
- Keep the fix small and focused to reduce rollout risk where possible; if a large refactor is required, capture it as a follow-up work item rather than expanding the scope here.
- Preserve existing slot-loading response semantics unless directly implicated by the root cause.

## Existing state
- Example log excerpt shows session invalidation and `history_mismatch` near the timestamp of interest (2026-06-05 22:44:34) and a 500 response for the POST to `/v1/chat/completions`.
- Primary codepaths to inspect: `proxy/proxy/server.py` (completions handler, session resolution, slot checks, fallback and error handling) and `proxy/proxy/session_manager.py` (session lifecycle, invalidation, restore detection).
- Existing tests that exercise session handling and backend resilience exist under `proxy/tests/` (`test_incremental_ingestion.py`, `test_session_manager.py`, `test_backend_resilience.py`) and should be extended.
- Related configuration and docs: `proxy/config.yaml`, `proxy/README.md` (session header usage, fallback reasons), and `proxy/MODEL_ADD.md`.

## Desired change (high-level)
1. Triage & reproduce: collect correlated logs/traces for the event window, reproduce the failing request locally or in staging where possible (same headers/session id), and isolate the failing code path.
2. Implement hotfix: make a small, well-tested change (e.g., add missing exception handling, guard race conditions in session invalidation, fix header propagation) that eliminates the 500 for the identified cause.
3. Test & verify: add unit/integration tests that cover the failure and run full test suite locally; run staged verification and (if approved) roll the fix to production with rollback steps.
4. Observability: add metrics/headers/logs to make recurrence easy to detect (for example increment a `proxy_5xx` or `session_fallback` metric when this path fails) and create an alert and a short runbook for on-call.
5. Document: update `proxy/README.md` and any runbook entries to describe the fix and any changed behaviours.

## Related work (manual seeds)
- proxy/proxy/server.py — primary handler for `/v1/chat/completions`; contains slot checks, session resolution, and fallback/error branches (primary change location).
- proxy/proxy/session_manager.py — session lifecycle and invalidation logic; logs `history_mismatch` and other fallback reasons; evidence indicates this module is involved.
- proxy/README.md — documents session headers and existing fallback behaviour; must be updated to reflect any observable header/metric changes or behaviour changes.
- Add session header resolution logging — LP-0MPOOT5P3007CZLF — completed; added logs verifying which session header was used (useful for correlating the provided example logs).
- Session ID generation and management core — LP-0MO6EWJ5K0088CBS — completed; session manager behaviour and TTL.
- Return informative slot-exhaustion responses / fallback features — LP-0MPQ2PLTC000GYRZ / LP-0MQ0O5IMA006KFKZ — related work on fallback and slot semantics; relevant for determining whether slot exhaustion plus fallback caused the 500s.
- Prior backend resilience and crash mitigation items — LP-0MPN8BALG000DBB3 and LP-0MPN836LQ002YQCY — contain historical context on backend crashes, retries and cascade behaviour.

## Related work (automated report)
- LP-0MPOOT5P3007CZLF — Add session header resolution logging (Completed). Adds explicit logs recording which session header was used; useful to correlate the example logs and confirm header sources.
- LP-0MO6EWJ5K0088CBS — Session ID generation and management core (Completed). Implements the SessionManager, TTL and header echo behaviour; relevant to session invalidation and restore logic.
- LP-0MPQ2PLTC000GYRZ — Return informative 503 / slot-exhaustion semantics (plan_complete). Contains prior design and test work for slot/exhaustion behaviour; consult to avoid conflicting client-facing semantics.
- LP-0MQ0O5IMA006KFKZ — Fallback to nearest remote free models (plan_complete). Describes fallback routing which may be implicated if fallback paths cause unexpected errors.
- LP-0MPN8BALG000DBB3 — Mitigate llama-server ROCm crash path and add backend recovery safeguards (Completed). Prior mitigation work for backend crashes and cascade failures.
- proxy/proxy/server.py (file) — Primary implementation of the completions handler, session resolution, slot checks and fallback/error branches: primary change location.
- proxy/proxy/session_manager.py (file) — Session lifecycle, invalidation and restore-detection code; logs `history_mismatch` and contains session invalidation paths.
- proxy/tests/test_incremental_ingestion.py, proxy/tests/test_backend_resilience.py, proxy/tests/test_session_manager.py — Existing test modules that should be extended to cover the identified failure path and to provide regression coverage.

Recommendation: start investigation with LP-0MPOOT5P3007CZLF and LP-0MO6EWJ5K0088CBS for logging and session lifecycle context, then consult LP-0MPQ2PLTC000GYRZ and LP-0MQ0O5IMA006KFKZ for slot/fallback semantics to avoid unintended client-visible changes.

---

## Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers
- Q: "Remediation expectation (choose one)
  A) Immediate remediation/hotfix in prod + PR (urgent)  
  B) Investigation → RCA with remediation plan and PR opened (no immediate hotfix)  
  C) Investigation-only for now; hand off remediation to implementation team" — Answer (user): "A) Immediate remediation/hotfix in prod + PR (urgent)". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

- Q: "Logs & access (choose one)
  A) You will attach/point me to proxy + upstream logs/traces for the window around 2026-06-05 22:44:34 (preferred)  
  B) I (agent) have access to the environment/log store — provide path/credentials (please specify)  
  C) I need help retrieving logs (I will provide details of the environment; please advise what to collect)" — Answer (user): "B - you have the locations already, it is just a standard run of the proxy script". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

Research performed by agent (summary)
- Searched Worklog for proxy-related items and inspected related items (LP-0MPOOT5P3007CZLF, LP-0MO6EWJ5K0088CBS, LP-0MPQ2PLTC000GYRZ, LP-0MQ0O5IMA006KFKZ). 
- Searched the repository for session, slots, and completions handlers and read `proxy/proxy/server.py`, `proxy/proxy/session_manager.py`, `proxy/README.md` and relevant tests under `proxy/tests/` to identify likely failure locations and existing tests to extend.

OPEN QUESTIONS / TBD
- Alert thresholds for monitoring/alerting (e.g., X 500s in Y minutes) — TBD; please confirm preferred thresholds if you want them specified in Acceptance Criteria.

<!-- Appendix end -->
